description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Closes 3 critical-review findings as a single merge-gate completeness bundle:

- **F3** (CRIT): No try/rescue around MutationLoop.run/1 — a raise during the loaded-mutation window exits run_one_site/3 with the mutated AST still installed as the current code for that module. No restore runs, no JSON emitted.
- **F4** (CRIT, Testing reviewer): lib/mutagen_ex.ex and test/mutagen_ex_test.exs are still the `mix new` hello-world placeholders. Not a runtime defect but a productionization gap that ships v1 with a credibility hit.
- **F27** (HIGH): The :compile_error branch in run_one_site/3 calls `_ = restore(...)` — restore failures on that branch are silently discarded. Surface them through the same channel as the success branch.

## Approach

Mirror the shape of `with_cover_lifecycle/2` (lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex:119-133). Extract a `with_restore/4` helper in lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:

```
defp with_restore(file_ast, %Site{} = site, cfg, fun) do
  try do
    result = fun.()
    case restore(file_ast, site.file, cfg) do
      :ok -> {:ok, result}
      {:error, msg} -> {:error, :restore_failed, msg}
    end
  rescue e ->
    _ = safe_restore(file_ast, site, cfg)
    reraise(e, __STACKTRACE__)
  catch kind, value ->
    _ = safe_restore(file_ast, site, cfg)
    :erlang.raise(kind, value, __STACKTRACE__)
  end
end
```

`safe_restore/3` is a thin wrapper around `restore/3` that catches its own raises (cleanup never throws — preserves the original stacktrace inside `rescue`).

Wrap the success-branch in `run_one_site/3` (lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:321-358) so the protected window spans from after `safe_compile_quoted` success through `MutationLoop.run/1` + `settle_code_server!/3` + result handling — until `restore/3` returns `:ok`.

Route the :compile_error branch (line 360-379) through the same helper so restore failures surface as `{:error, :unrecoverable_restore_failure, ...}` instead of `_ = restore(...)` swallowing.

Delete lib/mutagen_ex.ex (currently `mix new` placeholder) and test/mutagen_ex_test.exs (currently `mix new` test). The module either holds real content or is removed; nothing in the rest of lib/ imports it.

Revise .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md: add new requirement `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.rNN` (raise/throw/exit during loaded-mutation window MUST trigger restore before propagation; restore failure during such propagation MUST be reported but MUST NOT mask the original cause). Add verification stub.

Revise README "Known caveats" inline to remove any F3-class concerns.

## Verification

- New test file `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs` (or extend mutation_runner_test.exs) covering:
  - `raise` inside the protected window → original module bytecode restored, exception reaches caller verbatim
  - `throw` inside the protected window → same
  - `exit` inside the protected window → same
  - restore failure during raised window → surfaces as `:unrecoverable_restore_failure` without masking original cause
  - :compile_error branch restore failure → no longer silently discarded
- `mix test` (or strict pre-merge: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test`) green
- Spec verification stubs for the new pipeline requirement execute: true
- Spike-I / r6 bytecode-identity tests (already in suite) remain green — the change is structural, not behavioral on the happy path

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex — F8 (Process.put smuggle replacement) is bw .23, not .17
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (does not exist yet) — F2-architectural is bw .18
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex — F6/F7 are bw .20/.21
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex, test_selector.ex, baseline.ex, ast_cache.ex — AST/perf consolidation is bw .25
- mix.exs — supervision tree mod: declaration is bw .18

## Allowed touches

- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex.ex (delete)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs (or new mutation_runner_raise_test.exs)
- test/mutagen_ex_test.exs (delete)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md
- README.md (caveats section)
- CHANGELOG.md (entry for .17)

## Out of Scope (intent)

- Do not introduce a supervision tree or Application module (that is .18 architectural debt)
- Do not change MutationLoop.run/1 internal mechanics (Task setup, cancel modes, etc.) — only its caller behavior
- Do not change restore/3 or its tagged-tuple return shape; the new helper builds on top
- Do not broaden the protected window earlier than `safe_compile_quoted` success — there is nothing to restore before that point
- Preserve the original exception/stacktrace in rescue paths — `reraise/2` with `__STACKTRACE__`, not bare `raise`

## Merge gate

- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] New raise/throw/exit tests pass
- [ ] Spec verification for the new mutagen.mutation_pipeline requirement passes
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] README caveats updated; no stale F3 references
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE on this ticket
title: S9a: try/rescue around mutation window + delete mix-new placeholder + surface :compile_error restore failure
